# PRD: Phase 3 — Grants & Fundraising (Grants Search + Donor CRM)

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-17
**Status:** ✅ Approved by Citlali — ready for coding agent
**Requested by:** Citlali (final phase of tonight's "do all three" sprint)

---

## 1. Goal

Make the Development Director real. After this PRD, a user can chat with the Dev Director and:
- "Find grants for youth mentoring programs over $10K with deadlines in the next 60 days" → returns real federal grants from Grants.gov
- "Add Sarah Henderson as a donor, $5K gift on March 15" → creates a donor row + logs the gift in the org's CRM
- "Who are our top 5 donors this year?" → reads real CRM data from Supabase
- "Draft a thank-you email for our $10K+ donors who haven't been contacted in 90 days" → uses CRM to find the list, drafts the email

This is the most distinctive Edify feature in the roadmap. Phase 1 (foundation), 2a (OAuth), 2b (Calendar tools + tool-use loop) all set up the substrate. Phase 3 is where Edify starts feeling like a real organizational AI.

## 2. Why

Per the roadmap, Phase 3 is the "Grants & Fundraising" phase — Development Director gets external grant search, internal donor tracking, and the ability to reason across both ("which donor's giving history aligns with this grant funder's priorities?"). The roadmap puts Phase 3 after Phase 2 in priority order, but they're independent — Phase 3 doesn't need any Google integrations to be useful.

## 3. Architecture Decisions

- **Grants.gov REST API v2 directly, no SDK.** The `https://api.grants.gov/v1/api/search2` endpoint is publicly accessible — no auth required for basic search. `api.data.gov` key is OPTIONAL (raises rate limits when present). Use `process.env.API_DATA_GOV_KEY` if set; pass as `X-Api-Key` header.
- **CRM tables in Supabase, RLS-isolated per org.** Three new tables: `donors`, `donations`, `donor_interactions`. All filter by `org_id`, all use the same RLS pattern as `members` / `conversations`.
- **No external CRM integration.** No Salesforce, no DonorPerfect, no Bloomerang. Edify owns the donor data inside Supabase. Future PRDs can add import/export from external systems if needed; not in scope tonight.
- **Tools registered in existing `lib/tools/registry.ts`.** Phase 2b already built the per-archetype tool dispatch. We just add new tool families. Two new prefixes: `grants_*` (Grants.gov search) and `crm_*` (donor management).
- **Development Director gets all grants + crm tools. Programs Director gets read-only grants tools (can search for compliance research; can't write to CRM).** Other archetypes get nothing.
- **Currency stored as integer cents.** `donations.amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL`. Avoids floating-point money sins. Tools accept dollars from the user/Claude, convert to cents on write, format as dollars on read.

## 4. Scope

### In scope

1. **`apps/web/src/lib/grants-gov.ts`** (NEW) — typed wrappers around 2 Grants.gov REST endpoints:
   - `searchGrants({ keyword?, oppStatuses?, eligibilities?, fundingCategories?, agencies?, deadlineWithinDays?, rows?, sortBy? })` → `{ grants: Grant[], total: number }`
     - Calls POST `https://api.grants.gov/v1/api/search2`
     - `oppStatuses` defaults to `["forecasted","posted"]` (active opportunities only)
     - `rows` defaults to 20, hard-capped at 50 (to keep Claude context manageable)
     - `deadlineWithinDays`: convenience param — convert to a date filter on `closeDate`
     - `sortBy`: `'closeDate:asc'` (deadline soonest first) by default
   - `fetchGrantDetails({ opportunityId })` → `{ grant: GrantDetail }`
     - Calls POST `https://api.grants.gov/v1/api/fetchOpportunity`
     - Returns full description, eligibility narrative, attachments list, etc.
   - Both functions: include `X-Api-Key` header if `process.env.API_DATA_GOV_KEY` is set; omit otherwise
   - Throw `GrantsGovError` (custom) on non-2xx
   - `Grant` type: subset of the API response — `{ opportunityId, opportunityNumber, title, agency, status, postedDate, closeDate, awardCeiling, awardFloor, fundingInstrumentTypes, eligibilityCategories, opportunityCategoryExplanation }`
   - `GrantDetail` type: includes everything in `Grant` plus `description`, `additionalInformation`, `synopsisDescription`

2. **CRM migration `supabase/migrations/00016_crm_tables.sql`** (NEW). Append same to `combined_migration.sql`.
   ```sql
   -- Donors: people or organizations who have given (or might give) to the org
   create table donors (
     id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
     org_id uuid not null references orgs(id) on delete cascade,
     name text not null,
     email text,
     phone text,
     address text,
     donor_type text not null check (donor_type in ('individual', 'foundation', 'corporation', 'government', 'other')),
     notes text,
     tags text[] default '{}',
     first_gift_at timestamptz,
     last_gift_at timestamptz,
     lifetime_giving_cents bigint default 0 not null,
     created_at timestamptz default now() not null,
     updated_at timestamptz default now() not null,
     created_by uuid references members(id)
   );
   create index idx_donors_org on donors(org_id);
   create index idx_donors_email on donors(org_id, email) where email is not null;
   
   -- Donations: individual gifts
   create table donations (
     id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
     org_id uuid not null references orgs(id) on delete cascade,
     donor_id uuid not null references donors(id) on delete cascade,
     amount_cents bigint not null check (amount_cents > 0),
     given_at date not null,
     campaign text,
     designation text,
     payment_method text,
     receipt_sent boolean default false not null,
     notes text,
     created_at timestamptz default now() not null,
     created_by uuid references members(id)
   );
   create index idx_donations_org on donations(org_id);
   create index idx_donations_donor on donations(donor_id, given_at desc);
   
   -- Donor interactions: touchpoints (calls, emails, meetings, events attended)
   create table donor_interactions (
     id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
     org_id uuid not null references orgs(id) on delete cascade,
     donor_id uuid not null references donors(id) on delete cascade,
     interaction_type text not null check (interaction_type in ('email','phone','meeting','event','letter','other')),
     occurred_at timestamptz not null,
     summary text not null,
     follow_up_needed boolean default false not null,
     follow_up_at date,
     created_at timestamptz default now() not null,
     created_by uuid references members(id)
   );
   create index idx_interactions_org on donor_interactions(org_id);
   create index idx_interactions_donor on donor_interactions(donor_id, occurred_at desc);
   
   -- RLS: SELECT-only for members of the org. INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE go through service role per established pattern (see migration 00015).
   alter table donors enable row level security;
   alter table donations enable row level security;
   alter table donor_interactions enable row level security;
   
   create policy "donors_org_select" on donors for select using (
     org_id in (select org_id from members where user_id = auth.uid())
   );
   create policy "donations_org_select" on donations for select using (
     org_id in (select org_id from members where user_id = auth.uid())
   );
   create policy "interactions_org_select" on donor_interactions for select using (
     org_id in (select org_id from members where user_id = auth.uid())
   );
   
   -- Trigger: when a donation is inserted, update donors.lifetime_giving_cents + first/last_gift_at
   create or replace function update_donor_aggregates() returns trigger as $$
   begin
     update donors set
       lifetime_giving_cents = (select coalesce(sum(amount_cents), 0) from donations where donor_id = new.donor_id),
       first_gift_at = (select min(given_at) from donations where donor_id = new.donor_id),
       last_gift_at = (select max(given_at) from donations where donor_id = new.donor_id),
       updated_at = now()
     where id = new.donor_id;
     return new;
   end;
   $$ language plpgsql security definer;
   
   create trigger donations_update_aggregates
     after insert on donations
     for each row execute function update_donor_aggregates();
   ```

3. **`apps/web/src/lib/crm.ts`** (NEW) — typed Supabase wrappers:
   - `listDonors({ serviceClient, orgId, limit?, search?, tags?, sortBy? })` → `{ donors: Donor[] }`
   - `getDonor({ serviceClient, orgId, donorId })` → `{ donor: Donor, recentDonations: Donation[], recentInteractions: Interaction[] }`
   - `createDonor({ serviceClient, orgId, memberId, ...donorFields })` → `{ donor: Donor }`
   - `logDonation({ serviceClient, orgId, memberId, donorId, amountCents, givenAt, ...optional })` → `{ donation: Donation }` (the trigger handles aggregate updates)
   - `logInteraction({ serviceClient, orgId, memberId, donorId, ...fields })` → `{ interaction: Interaction }`
   - All functions enforce `org_id` filter on every query (defense in depth — RLS bypassed by service client)

4. **`apps/web/src/lib/tools/grants.ts`** (NEW) — Anthropic tool defs + dispatcher for grants:
   - `grants_search` (description: "Search federal grant opportunities on Grants.gov by keyword, deadline, eligibility, etc. Use when the user asks about funding opportunities, grants for X cause, deadlines in next N days...")
   - `grants_get_details` ("Fetch the full text and details of a specific grant opportunity. Use after grants_search when the user wants more info on one entry.")
   - `executeGrantsTool({ name, input })` → `{ content, is_error? }`

5. **`apps/web/src/lib/tools/crm.ts`** (NEW) — Anthropic tool defs + dispatcher for CRM:
   - `crm_list_donors` ("List donors in the org's CRM. Optional filters: search (name/email), tags, sort by lifetime_giving / last_gift / name. Returns up to 25.")
   - `crm_get_donor` ("Get full details of one donor including recent donations and interactions. Use after crm_list_donors when the user asks about a specific person.")
   - `crm_create_donor` ("Add a new donor to the CRM. Required: name, donor_type. Optional: email, phone, address, notes, tags.")
   - `crm_log_donation` ("Record a donation/gift. Required: donor_id (from list_donors), amount in dollars, given_at (ISO date). Optional: campaign, designation, payment_method, notes.")
   - `crm_log_interaction` ("Record a touchpoint with a donor. Required: donor_id, interaction_type (email/phone/meeting/event/letter/other), occurred_at, summary. Optional: follow_up_needed, follow_up_at.")
   - `executeCrmTool({ name, input, orgId, memberId, serviceClient })` → `{ content, is_error? }`
   - Dollar→cents conversion happens INSIDE the dispatcher (Claude says "5000", we store 500000 cents)

6. **Update `apps/web/src/lib/tools/registry.ts`**:
   - Add `grants_*` and `crm_*` dispatch — register `executeGrantsTool` and `executeCrmTool` (use a Map per the pattern set up; if registry currently uses startsWith, extend with else-if branches — same simple shape Phase 2b used)
   - Update `ARCHETYPE_TOOLS`:
     - `development_director`: `[...grantsTools, ...crmTools]`
     - `programs_director`: `[...grantsTools]` (read-only on grants for compliance research; no CRM access)
     - `executive_assistant`, `events_director`: unchanged (calendar tools)
     - `marketing_director`, `hr_volunteer_coordinator`: unchanged (empty)
   - System prompt addendum constants for `GRANTS_TOOLS_ADDENDUM` and `CRM_TOOLS_ADDENDUM` — exported from their respective `lib/tools/*.ts` files. Route concatenates whichever applies.

7. **System prompt construction in `team/[slug]/chat/route.ts`**:
   - Currently appends `CALENDAR_TOOLS_ADDENDUM` (after the Phase 2b /simplify, this is imported from `lib/tools/calendar.ts`)
   - Now: build addendum based on what tools the archetype actually has. Concatenate the appropriate addendums. Keep the conditional simple — no template registry needed for 3 addendums.

8. **CRM dashboard UI** — out of scope. The CRM is reachable only via Dev Director chat tonight. Building a `/dashboard/donors` page is its own UX work, separate PRD.

### Out of scope

- Bulk import of donors from CSV
- Email campaign integration (would touch SendGrid — Phase 4)
- Grant application submission (requires SAM.gov UEI, Edify org incorporation — long-blocked)
- Saved grant searches / alerts (per-client, deferred)
- CRM dashboard UI (separate PRD)
- Donor segmentation queries beyond what tools naturally enable
- Reports / analytics / charts
- Pledge tracking (vs. realized gifts) — current schema is realized only
- Multi-currency
- Tax receipt generation
- Donor merge / dedup tooling

## 5. Acceptance Criteria

Done when:
1. ✅ User chats with Development Director and asks "Find grants for early childhood education with deadlines in the next 90 days" → real Grants.gov results returned with title, deadline, amount range
2. ✅ User asks "Add Acme Foundation as a donor, foundation type, contact john@acme.org" → real row in `donors` table, returned to user with confirmation
3. ✅ User asks "Log a $10,000 gift from Acme on April 1" → row in `donations`, donor's `lifetime_giving_cents` updated by trigger
4. ✅ User asks "Who are our top donors this year?" → real list from CRM
5. ✅ Programs Director can search grants but can NOT modify CRM (verifiable: `crm_*` tools not in their tool array)
6. ✅ Marketing/HR/EA/Events directors don't have grants or CRM tools (each has only what their phase wired in)
7. ✅ `npm run build` clean
8. ✅ Single commit on main, message starting `feat: Grants search + Donor CRM (Phase 3)`

## 6. Risks & Open Questions

- **Grants.gov rate limits.** Without an api.data.gov key the limit is ~1k req/hour per IP. With a key, much higher. We have a key in `.env.local` (added 2026-04-17). The agent should add `API_DATA_GOV_KEY` to the Citlali Vercel env-var checklist to enable higher limits in production.
- **Migration apply order.** This is migration 00016. Citlali still hasn't applied 00009-00015 (they're queued for tomorrow in `combined_migration.sql`). The combined file gets 00016 appended too. Recommend Citlali run the full combined file in one go.
- **No auth on the Grants.gov API.** The agent should NOT pass any user-identifying info in those calls. Pure search queries only.
- **Donor PII.** The CRM stores names, emails, phones, addresses, donation amounts. RLS ensures cross-org isolation. No client-side encryption tonight (could be Phase-later if compliance requires). Service role client respects org_id filters in code.
- **Tool count climbing.** Dev Director now has 7 tools (2 grants + 5 CRM). Programs Director has 2. Claude generally handles 10+ tools fine, but tool descriptions must stay distinct enough to avoid confusion. Write them carefully.

## 7. Estimated Effort

~30 min agent + ~10 min /simplify pass.

- [x] Citlali approved (2026-04-17 ~11:01 PM EDT, with the "do all three" decision)
- [x] Architecture pre-locked (REST direct, in-Supabase CRM, 3 tables with trigger, dollars-to-cents)
- [ ] Lopmon spawns coding agent
